title: fix: stop /video/models falling through to video generation
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Registers `GET /video/models` on the same describeRoute + handler as `/image/models` (video models are already listed there), so the path no longer falls through to the `/video/:prompt` generation route and burns GPU on an unauthenticated request
- Adds `/video/models` to the optional-auth middleware block, matching the other models endpoints
- Extends the existing public-model-path routing test with `/video/models`

Fixes #8995

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: No more 'models' movies
gist.blurb: Fixed a quirky routing bug where asking for the list of video models accidentally generated a video about 'models'. The /video/models endpoint now correctly returns the JSON list you actually wanted.
gist.summary: Fixed a routing issue where GET /video/models fell through to the video generation endpoint, returning a generated video instead of the model list.
gist.impact: Developers querying /video/models will now correctly receive the JSON array of available video models instead of triggering an unauthenticated video generation.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a traffic director or fixing a signpost, ensuring requests for 'models' go to a data list rather than a film reel. Nomnom creature looking at a confusing film strip.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny traffic cop hat, fixing a wooden signpost in a digital garden. One sign points to a glowing text document, the other to a film reel. The round tan nomnom creature sits nearby, looking confused at a film strip. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
